generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains IncubatorBox, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, ControllerLogic
    base:isContainedIn SystemUnderStudy
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Controlled via MOSFETs and optocouplers to deliver power (minimum 100W) for heating the air inside the styrofoam box. Raspberry Pi 4 triggers via GPIO signals."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Regulated through GPIO commands to adjust airflow (0.02 m³/s) for uniform temperature distribution. Voltage can be dynamically set (12V/18V) to influence thermal mixing."
]

instance ControllerLogic : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Implemented as a statechart-based bang-bang controller with parameters: LL (Lower Limit), UL (Upper Limit), H (Heating Duration), C (Waiting Duration)."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance RoomTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 temperature sensor measuring room temperature (T_room) via 1-Wire bus."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RoomTemperatureData
]

instance AirTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First internal DS18S20 temperature sensor (T1) monitoring air temperature at initial central position."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirTemperatureData1
]

instance AirTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal DS18S20 temperature sensor (T2) initially placed centrally, later optimized to hottest spot."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirTemperatureData2
]

instance AirTemperatureSensor3 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Third internal DS18S20 temperature sensor (T3) initially peripheral, later optimized to coldest spot."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirTemperatureData3
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance RoomTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RoomTemperatureSensor
]

instance AirTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirTemperatureSensor1
]

instance AirTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirTemperatureSensor2
]

instance AirTemperatureData3 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirTemperatureSensor3
]

instance ControlCommandData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Binary control signals (GPIO states) for heat/fan ON/OFF commands sent via RabbitMQ."
]

instance PowerConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Voltage (V) and current (I) measurements for heatbed/fan power consumption via RabbitMQ."
]

// C5: Data Sources
instance RoomTemperatureValues : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings (K/°C) from RoomTemperatureSensor via RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RoomTemperatureData
]

instance AirTemperatureValues1 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings (K/°C) from AirTemperatureSensor1 via RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirTemperatureData1
]

instance AirTemperatureValues2 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings (K/°C) from AirTemperatureSensor2 via RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirTemperatureData2
]

instance AirTemperatureValues3 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings (K/°C) from AirTemperatureSensor3 via RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirTemperatureData3
]

instance ControlSignals : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Binary GPIO states (ON/OFF) for heat/fan actuators via RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControlCommandData
]

instance PowerMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time power consumption data (V, I) for actuators via RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PowerConsumptionData
]

instance ExperimentalDataPlots : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature uniformity plots (figs. 10-12) used for model validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RabbitMQEnabler
]

// C6: Models
instance ModelATemperature : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "First-order differential equation model (eq. 5) with parameters *C_air* (J/K) and *G_box* (J/K) to predict temperature changes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ScipyOptimizationEnabler
]

instance ModelBTemperature : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Coupled differential equations model (eqs. 9-10) with parameters *C_air* (J/K), *C_heater* (J/K), *G_box* (J/K), *G_heater* (J/K) accounting for heatbed thermal inertia."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ScipyOptimizationEnabler
]

// C7: Enablers
instance RabbitMQEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time message broker enabling bidirectional communication between physical twin and digital twin via RabbitMQ on Raspberry Pi."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataExchangeService
]

instance ScipyOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python Scipy implementation for non-linear least squares optimization to refine model parameters."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCalibrationService
]

instance GPIOController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-level drivers for GPIO communication with heatbed/fan actuators."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlLogicService
]

// C8: Services
instance DataExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature prediction and actuator command synchronization via RabbitMQ."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperaturePredictionInsight, ControlCommandAction
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Non-linear least squares optimization to minimize residual error between model predictions and experimental data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterRefinementInsight
]

instance ControlLogicService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Statechart-based bang-bang controller with temperature regulation parameters."
    DTDFVocab:provides StabilityOptimizationInsight
]

instance ThermalEfficiencyAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implicit energy analysis of temperature regulation performance (e.g., 200J required for 7.85K temperature rise)."
    DTDFVocab:provides EfficiencyMetricsInsight
]

// C9: Insights/Actions
instance TemperaturePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimates temperature behavior under varying conditions using Model A (2 parameters) and Model B (4 parameters)."
]

instance ParameterRefinementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Calibrated model parameters (*C_air*, *G_box*, *C_heater*, *G_heater*) improve predictive accuracy via gradient descent optimization."
]

instance StabilityOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) are optimized for stable temperature regulation."
]

instance EfficiencyMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Thermal efficiency metrics derived from energy consumption patterns and temperature uniformity."
]

instance ControlCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic ON/OFF commands for heatbed/fan actuators based on temperature thresholds and statechart logic."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemUnderStudy : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "A thermal incubator system designed for maintaining and regulating temperature within an insulated styrofoam container."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance IncubatorBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated styrofoam box (volume: 0.03 m³) for thermal isolation containing the heatbed and sensors."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** is implemented via:
  - **RabbitMQ message broker**: The DT sends commands (e.g., heatbed/fan ON/OFF) to the Raspberry Pi’s low-level driver,
  - **Control protocol**: The controller (statechart) interprets messages and issues GPIO commands to:
    - **Heatbed**: Toggle via MOSFETs/optocouplers (4A current isolation),
    - **Fan**: Adjust voltage (12V/24V) via GPIO,
  - **Latency**: Implicitly tied to RabbitMQ message processing time (assumed <100ms for real-time control)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **twinning time scale** is defined by:
  - **Controller loop rate**: Implicitly tied to the **statechart execution cycle** (not quantified, but assumed <1s for bang-bang control),
  - **Model update frequency**: Calibration occurs during **experimental runs** (figs. 4.1–4.2) with no explicit sampling rate specified,
  - **Data synchronization**: RabbitMQ ensures **real-time (<100ms) message passing** between PT and DT,
  - **Thermal dynamics**: Temperature changes are modeled with **continuous-time differential equations** (eqs. 4–9), where time steps align with physical actuation cycles (e.g., 2s for air circulation)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as a **centralized architecture** with the following components:
  - **Physical Twin (PT)**: Insulated incubator with sensors/actuators (figs. 2–5),
  - **Digital Twin (DT)**: Implemented as a **RabbitMQ-based service** that:
    - Receives temperature data from PT via RabbitMQ,
    - Executes model predictions (Model A/B) and calibration algorithms,
    - Sends control commands (heat/fan) back to PT via RabbitMQ,
  - **Communication layer**: RabbitMQ ensures **real-time bidirectional data flow** between PT and DT,
  - **Orchestration**: The controller (Raspberry Pi) acts as the **intermediary**, translating DT commands into GPIO signals for actuation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The incubator DT operates across the following **life-cycle stages**:
  - **Design phase**: Models (Model A/B) are developed with assumptions (e.g., uniform temperature distribution, constant air heat capacity),
  - **Manufactured phase**: Physical components (styrofoam box, heatbed, sensors) are built and calibrated (e.g., sensor placement in fig. 11),
  - **Operated phase**: Real-time control via statechart-based controller (fig. 8) with RabbitMQ for data exchange,
  - **Model validation**: Experimental results (figs. 10–12) validate assumptions (e.g., temperature uniformity, fan airflow effects)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **fidelity and validity considerations** include:
  - **Model assumptions**:
    - **Assumption 1**: Temperature uniformity is validated via experiments (max discrepancy ≤7°C at 12V, ≤6°C at 18V fan voltage),
    - **Assumption 2**: Box walls are assumed non-heating (verified via experiments),
    - **Assumption 3**: Constant air heat capacity (700 J/kg·K) is justified,
    - **Assumption 4**: Heatbed/fan heat losses are ignored (Model B relaxes this),
  - **Validation mechanisms**:
    - **Experimental data** (figs. 10–12) compares model predictions with real-time measurements,
    - **Non-linear least squares optimization** minimizes residual error between model outputs and data,
  - **Uncertainty**: Model parameters (*C_air*, *G_box*, etc.) are calibrated to reduce prediction errors but may still introduce approximation errors due to simplified assumptions."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network connection** between PT and DT uses:
  - **Protocol**: **RabbitMQ message broker** for real-time data exchange,
  - **Communication channels**:
    - **Sensors**: 1-Wire bus (DS18S20) for temperature readings,
    - **Actuators**: GPIO signals for heatbed/fan control,
    - **Message format**: Binary control commands (ON/OFF) and temperature values (K/°C) in JSON-like structures,
  - **Latency**: Implicitly <100ms for RabbitMQ-based synchronization,
  - **Architecture**: **Centralized** with Raspberry Pi as the bridge between PT and DT."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is **deployed on-premises** via:
  - **Hosting platform**: Raspberry Pi 4 Model B (embedded system) running:
    - **RabbitMQ server** (local deployment for message broker),
    - **Python Scipy** for model calibration (local execution),
    - **Low-level drivers** for sensor/actuator communication,
  - **Network**: Built-in Wi-Fi/Bluetooth for wireless connectivity,
  - **Open-source access**: Full hardware/software specifications available at [GitHub](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The DT includes the following **security and safety considerations**:
  - **Safety protocols**:
    - **Low-level driver** enforces safe states for actuators (e.g., heatbed/fan limits),
    - **Temperature limits** (LL, UL) prevent overheating/undereating,
  - **Cybersecurity**:
    - RabbitMQ is deployed on the Raspberry Pi (local network) to mitigate remote attacks,
    - GPIO signals are isolated via MOSFETs/optocouplers to prevent electrical hazards,
  - **Remote operation**: Not explicitly detailed, but the system is designed for **local deployment** with no mention of remote execution risks (e.g., human accidents)."
]
